text: {{c1::Task success}} measures whether the agent completed the intended objective, not merely whether it produced fluent text.
explanation: It is the most important metric for production agent workflows.
usage: Used in coding agents, support agents, and automated workflow systems.
spanish: El task success mide si el agente completó el objetivo previsto, no solo si produjo texto fluido.

text: {{c1::Tool}} call accuracy measures whether the agent selected and used the correct tool at the right time.
explanation: It is essential for tool-using and action-oriented agents.
usage: Critical in search, database, and coding assistants.
spanish: La precisión de tool calls mide si el agente seleccionó y usó la herramienta correcta en el momento adecuado.

text: {{c1::Hallucination rate}} tracks how often an agent makes unsupported claims or invents facts.
explanation: It is one of the most important safety and trust metrics for agents.
usage: Critical for knowledge agents, legal assistants, and enterprise copilots.
spanish: La tasa de alucinación mide cuán a menudo un agente hace afirmaciones no sustentadas o inventa hechos.

text: {{c1::Trace logging}} captures the full path of an agent run, including prompts, tool calls, intermediate outputs, and final decisions.
explanation: Without traces, debugging agent behavior becomes guesswork.
usage: Essential in production agent orchestration and debugging.
spanish: El trace logging captura el camino completo de una ejecución de agente, incluyendo prompts, tool calls, outputs intermedios y decisiones finales.

text: {{c1::Failure modes}} include loops, wrong tool selection, premature termination, and policy violations.
explanation: Classifying failures makes it possible to improve reliability systematically.
usage: Used in agent benchmarking, safety reviews, and incident analysis.
spanish: Los failure modes incluyen bucles, selección incorrecta de herramientas, terminación prematura y violaciones de política.

text: {{c1::Human}}-in-the-loop systems ask for approval or intervention when the agent is uncertain, risky, or high-impact.
explanation: It is a practical safeguard for enterprise deployment.
usage: Used in finance, healthcare, and customer support operations.
spanish: Los sistemas human-in-the-loop piden aprobación o intervención cuando el agente está incierto, es riesgoso o de alto impacto.

explanation:
Desglose de variables:
  • \(n\): Total number of generated code samples per problem.
  • \(c\): Number of correct generated code samples that pass unit tests.
  • \(k\): Number of samples selected to determine success probability.
  • \(\binom{\cdot}{\cdot}\): Binomial coefficient.
spanish: Fórmula de pass@k Coding Evaluation Formula

text:
Pregunta: What is the core execution mechanism of SWE-bench (Jimenez et al., 2024) to evaluate code generation agents?
Opciones:
  • A) Running agent-generated code patches within isolated Docker containers containing repository states and verifying unit test execution success
  • B) Comparing generated python strings using token-matching BLEU scores
  • C) Statically parsing agent ASTs to verify syntax validation rules
  • D) Prompting Claude to evaluate agent code on a scale of 1 to 10

Respuesta correcta: {{c1::A) Running agent-generated code patches within isolated Docker containers containing repository states and verifying unit test execution success}}
explanation: Justificación: SWE-bench evaluates agent patches by executing them inside specialized Docker environments containing the exact codebase state. It runs pre-existing unit tests to check if the patch resolves the targeted GitHub issue without breaking other functionalities.
usage: Quiz assertion: A) Running agent-generated code patches within isolated Docker containers containing repository states and verifying unit test execution success
spanish: Quiz: What is the core execution mechanism of SWE-bench (Jimenez et al., 2024) to evaluate code generation agents?

text:
Pregunta: Which set of systematic biases must be mitigated when using an LLM as a judge to evaluate pairwise model generations?
Opciones:
  • A) Position bias (order effects), Verbosity bias (favoring longer outputs), and Self-enhancement bias
  • B) Quantization noise, stochastic token dropouts, and beam search decay
  • C) Underfitting bias, covariate distribution shift, and validation leakages
  • D) Temperature scale differences, context window decay, and tokenizer compression loss

Respuesta correcta: {{c1::A) Position bias (order effects), Verbosity bias (favoring longer outputs), and Self-enhancement bias}}
explanation: Justificación: LLMs judging pairwise outputs suffer from Position bias (preferring whichever output is presented first), Verbosity bias (giving higher scores to longer, more detailed-looking responses even if they contain errors), and Self-enhancement bias (preferring responses generated by their own model weights). Mitigation includes swapping order and averaging evaluations.
usage: Quiz assertion: A) Position bias (order effects), Verbosity bias (favoring longer outputs), and Self-enhancement bias
spanish: Quiz: Which set of systematic biases must be mitigated when using an LLM as a judge to evaluate pairwise model generations?
